sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after clicking on the top yellow object. I need to compare the before and after frames to see if anything meaningful changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before/after for the yellow object click
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Check yellow objects
before_yellow = [n for n in before.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y']
after_yellow = [n for n in after.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y']

print("Before yellow:")
for n in before_yellow:
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
print("After yellow:")
for n in after_yellow:
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check blue bar
before_bar = [n for n in before.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] > 10]
after_bar = [n for n in after.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] > 10]
print("\nBlue bar before:", [(n['id'], n['pixels']) for n in before_bar])
print("Blue bar after:", [(n['id'], n['pixels']) for n in after_bar])

# Check gray markers
before_gray = [n for n in before.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w']
after_gray = [n for n in after.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w']
print("\nGray markers before:", [(n['id'], n['pixels'], n['boundary']) for n in before_gray])
print("Gray markers after:", [(n['id'], n['pixels'], n['boundary']) for n in after_gray])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the yellow object didn't change anything meaningful - only the blue bar timer decreased (60->59). The yellow objects and gray markers stayed exactly the same.
So far:
- Clicking the blue circle: timer decreases, checkerboard inverts
- Clicking the top yellow object: timer decreases, nothing else changes
Let me try clicking on the gray markers below the checkerboard. There are 3 marker positions (left, center, right). Each has a horizontal bar and a vertical bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the left gray marker (horizontal bar at row 42, col 25-27)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 42, 'col': 26}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
